name: ExtensionPaymentReconciliation_Allocation_Target
title: R5: Subject of the payment (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `PaymentReconciliation.allocation.target` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`PaymentReconciliation.allocation.target` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Claim,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Account,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Invoice,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ChargeItem,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Encounter,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Contract)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `PaymentReconciliation.allocation.target` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Claim,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Account,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Invoice,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ChargeItem,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Encounter,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Contract)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `PaymentReconciliation.allocation.target` has a context of PaymentReconciliation.detail based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `PaymentReconciliation`.
Element `PaymentReconciliation.allocation.target` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: Specific resource to which the payment/adjustment/advance applies.
